title: Kubernetes GitOps Platform
description: Full GitOps for Kubernetes: declarative env definition stored in git, auto-sync every 30s, rollback on drift, per-environment policy gates (Prod blocks WIP branches), secrets encrypted via sops+age.
features: Declarative env spec stored in git — single source of truth
FluxCD auto-sync every 30 seconds with drift detection
Per-environment branch policy — Prod merges only from release/*
Secrets encrypted via sops+age — no plaintext secrets in repo
benefits: Drift between git and cluster is detected and fixed in under 90 seconds
One git PR deploys to every environment with the same review process
A field engineer can promote a release in <5 minutes
Audit trail per environment change is the git commit itself

title: Hybrid + Multi-Cloud Networking
description: Zero-trust networking across on-prem/edge/cloud: cloud-router fabric connects VPCs across AWS/GCP/Azure, on-prem data-centre via WireGuard or IPSec, global anycast front-door with WAF.
features: Cloud-router fabric — single control-plane connects up to 40 VPCs/accounts
Site-to-cloud connectivity via WireGuard tunnel or IPSec with automated failover
Global anycast front-door with WAF + geo-ip routing per user
Zero-trust per-connection IAM authorization beyond the network boundary
benefits: Cost-optimised networking saves $50K/yr on cross-region bandwidth vs. raw cloud provider costs
DR to a lower-cost region in under 60 minutes
Apply security policy once at the edge and enforce everywhere
Team can use the same VPC-IDs across dev/staging/prod in any cloud or region

title: Serverless API Platform
description: Deploy any REST/GraphQL/WebSocket API in a single command: framework-agnostic cold-start elimination, per-request billing with 99.999% SLA, auto-edge-cache, built-in auth (Clerk/Auth0) and observability.
features: Framework-agnostic deploy — Node, Python, Go, Rust runtimes supported
Cold-start killer: pre-warmed containers eliminate cold starts
Per-request billing; 99.999% uptime SLA with financial credit guarantee
Auto-edge-cache with Redis-compatible semantics, built-in auth path and per-request logs
benefits: Deploy in one command — from code-test to production in under 10 minutes
Bill per 1ms instead of per hour — can save 90%+ on idle capacity costs
Edge-cache hits are auto-invalidated on data change
Built-in observability means you don't instrument on top — it's included

title: Data Lakehouse Platform
description: Open-source S3-compatible lakehouse with ACID transactions, SQL query engine (Presto/Trino), Open-Delta format, built-in catalog, automatic table partitioning, ML-ready with feature-store and model registry.
features: S3-compatible storage with ACID commit-log and Open-Delta table format
Unified SQL engine (Presto/Trino) across lake and warehouse tables
Built-in catalog + automatic partition discovery and data masking
Feature-store and model registry for ML pipelines on same platform
benefits: Stop paying two bills — data lake + warehouse in one platform
ACID means no partial-commit corruption even during failover
SQL analytics and ML model serving on the same co-located tables
Self-serve access through federated query engines without raw-S3 credentials

title: Chaos Engineering Platform
description: Chaos engineering at enterprise scale: experiment library (network-lat/pod-kill/disk-full), automated game-day runner, blast-radius limiter, post-mortem storage per experiment and evidence for compliance.
features: Curated experiment library — network latency, pod kill, disk full, DNS poisoning
Automated game-day runner with blast-radius limiter per experiment
Evidence storage — every run recorded, rendered, searchable with screenshot + metric traces
CI/CD gate capability — fail build if resilience threshold not achieved
benefits: Know your system's breaking-point before an incident finds it in production
CI/CD feedback means you catch resilience regressions in the same PR that causes them
Automated game-day means no more manual 'chaos afternoons'
Evidence stored cadence gives you one-click compliance report per audit

title: Kubernetes Cost Optimizer
description: K8s cost visibility and action: per-namespace per-pod autoscaling, unused node/claim reclaim, idle-developer-environment auto-stop, spot-instance placement, monthly budget vs actual drift alerting across clusters.
features: Per-namespace per-pod right-sizing recommendation with 85%+ adoption
Unused node and persistent-volume claim reclaim with zero-work approval
Idle developer environment auto-stop — clusters spin down after configurable idle period
Spot-instance placement engine for stateless workloads across 3 K8s clusters
benefits: Typically reduce cloud spend 35-45% within 90 days
Reclaim unused resources without stopping services or losing data
Developers get dev namespaces that stop at 6 PM without manual process
Spot instance auto-placement makes stateless pods cost half on average
